behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: A pure unit test of prompt-builder logic can miss wiring failures (e.g., caller forgets to pass taxonomy, serializer drops a field). Wiring the classifier module to the real API-client layer and asserting the emitted request catches those realistic boundary failures without needing real external API calls.
behaviors.test_description: Run the classifier against a test double API client/transport that captures outbound payloads end-to-end through the module->client boundary. Assert every call includes the full configured taxonomy label set (no missing/extra labels, correct field location/format).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: The failure is often at response parsing + mapping boundaries, not just in a local mapper function. A pure unit test could pass while the wired path still forwards raw API output. Integration coverage of the full response-handling path is the minimum robust level.
behaviors.test_description: Use a wired classifier+client path with a controlled API response containing an out-of-taxonomy label. Verify returned result is exactly "unknown" and that the unexpected label is not exposed to callers.

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model output stability. In-process mocks are typically deterministic and can hide true nondeterminism from the external AI model/settings. To catch real failures, the test must exercise real model calls repeatedly.
behaviors.test_description: Call the production classification path on the same document multiple times against the real AI API (e.g., 20-100 trials, fixed config). Assert the returned label is stable across runs; fail on observed label drift.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Sample size should be large enough to expose intermittent model variance.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is provider token accounting/model tokenizer behavior over real API calls. Local mocks or estimators may systematically undercount and hide budget breaches. This is infrastructure-dependent but not inherently LLM-nondeterminism dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Execute classification for representative documents against the real API and read actual token usage from provider response metadata/billing fields. Assert each call is <= configured token budget, with a guard margin if needed.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: A pure in-process mock can hide real HTTP status/header handling and retry timing behavior. Using real HTTP interactions (with a controllable test server) is the minimum level that reliably catches transport/protocol retry bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Run classifier against a local HTTP test server that first returns rate-limit response (e.g., 429 with Retry-After) then success. Assert classifier waits/backoffs, retries, and ultimately returns a successful label instead of surfacing an immediate error.
